name: symphonee-mind-retrieval-v1
description: Frozen known-item retrieval gold set for THE EVAL. Each query is a natural-language question whose answer lives in a specific Mind node (notes have stable content-derived ids). Questions are paraphrased - they deliberately do NOT echo the note title verbatim - so retrieval is genuinely tested and there is headroom for the Stage-2 activation kernel to prove (or fail to prove) it beats the RRF baseline. Difficulty is a hint only, not used in scoring. DO NOT edit relevance to chase a score: this set is FROZEN. Add new queries with new ids; never retune existing ones.

queries.question: where is the sequenced, staged build with go/no-go gates for shipping the next major version of Symphonee?
which note lays out the brain design - activation, spreading, the settle loop?
what's the plan to pull the knowledge graph out into its own standalone served component with a clean seam?
where did we write the long-term vision for where this product is heading - the cognitive substrate idea?
how do we check that the multi-CLI instruction system stays coherent and self-heals?
what has to happen before DYOB3 is production ready?
how does the upload-photo path differ from the designer path in DYOB3?
why was the DYOB3 render-contract branch abandoned and rolled back?
where's the reference for which API endpoints moved where during the restructure?
what did the end-to-end audit of the DYOB3 AI generation workflow uncover?
how do we smoke-test the API auth token?
what's the plan to port SocratiCode?
where's the audit covering zero-loss restoration and self-healing?
what was Claude's proposed layering approach for the architecture?
how should the shared instructions base file be slimmed down?
